content.claim: Hungary's Magyar Falu Program (Village Program) has provided 600 localities with 10 billion forints (EUR 28.3 million) for communal development, and PM Orbán has personally framed rural revival as a governing narrative.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Program targets rural infrastructure generally, not schools specifically
No evidence that community-space investments translate into school-specific protection
Narrative framing does not guarantee resource allocation to school retention
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
